_comment: Rules derived from traditional Marathi/North Indian panchang. App computes these from panchang-ts; day content files can override with 'override_rules: true'.

Shukla Tritiya.notes: Auspicious day — Akha Teej falls here

Shukla Chaturthi.notes: Vinayak Chaturthi — sacred to Lord Ganesha

Shukla Saptami.notes: Sapta means seven — some traditions avoid haircut

Shukla Ashtami.notes: Sacred to Durga Mata; avoid in Navratri context

Shukla Navami.notes: Navami is Devi's tithi — considered inauspicious for haircut

Shukla Ekadashi.notes: Ekadashi vrat — complete fast or grain-free fast observed

Shukla Dwadashi.notes: Dwadashi follows Ekadashi; parana (fast-break) day — remain sattvic

Shukla Trayodashi.notes: Pradosha Vrata on Trayodashi — those fasting avoid non-veg

Shukla Chaturdashi.notes: Shiva's tithi — Maha Shivaratri falls here; avoid meat

Purnima.notes: Full moon — sacred, auspicious for charity; avoid haircut and meat

Krishna Chaturthi.notes: Sankashti Chaturthi — Ganesha vrat; complete fast till moonrise

Krishna Ashtami.notes: Krishna Janmashtami falls here; sacred to Krishna

Krishna Ekadashi.notes: Ekadashi vrat — complete fast or grain-free fast observed

Krishna Chaturdashi.notes: Maha Shivaratri tithi; Narak Chaturdashi on Kartik Krishna Chaturdashi

Amavasya.notes: New moon — Pitru Tarpan day; sacred to ancestors; avoid haircut and meat
